gate_results.log_path: The DoD's cross-process integration test previously ran in NO gate (excluded from apps/api unit project by the integration-test suffix; not under tests/integration so pnpm test:integration skipped it). Relocated to tests/integration/ai-monitoring-redis-bridge.test.ts — now executed by the CI Integration Tests lane (ci.yml: Redis service + REDIS_URL) and by pre-ship integration-tests. Verified locally: 5/5 pass vs live Redis.

T-I1 drives the REAL RedisMonitoringPublisher.tick() (ai-worker) writing real ai-mon:v1:{tenant}:{kind} keys from canned AIMonitoringEvent rows, read back by the REAL RedisAIMonitoringStore (api) over real Redis; asserts source='redis' + published ROI cost 10/value 15. The old test could only hand-roll a payload (could not import across the api/ai-worker package boundary in the apps/api unit project).

describe.skipIf(!REDIS_URL) + a beforeAll ping probe with per-it ctx.skip() when unreachable. Verified: REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6399 (no listener) -> 5 skipped, exit 0, no hang (retryStrategy:()=>null fails fast). Runs+asserts where Redis is up (CI 6379, local intelliflow-redis-test 6380).

redis-store.ts SHA256 aa2f4464... and redis-monitoring-publisher.ts SHA256 b927a18d... are IDENTICAL to the pre-existing #59 attestation — the bridge code is unchanged. Diff is one new test file + one deleted orphan test + ioredis root devDep + a dependency-chain marker flip + session evidence.

Investigated the drift/hallucination limit->DB 'dead path'; it is a deliberate Lens-1 decision (unit.test.ts:412-420,494-500 assert limit->source:db) because the DB queries all-time (AIMonitoringService.ts:159-162) vs the publisher's rolling-window snapshot. Reversing it would re-introduce a query-semantic inconsistency. No code changed; no debt incurred (behavior is correct).

node scripts/pre-ship.mjs => pre-ship: PASS at the final committed SHA (mirrors CI required checks: format/lint/typecheck/unit/integration/coverage/build/security). codex-review PASS x3 consecutive standalone.

kpi_results.actual: Now ACTUALLY verified end-to-end: the real publisher writes the shared tenant-namespaced Redis snapshot the real store reads (T-I1), in a gate that runs (previously the verification ran nowhere). Multi-pod consistency holds by construction (publisher reads the shared Postgres tier, not per-pod singletons).
T-I2 asserts p95 < 50ms over 100 real store.getStatus reads vs live Redis — now executed in the integration lane.
T-I4 (tamper: garbage over valid -> source=db) + the existing Zod read-validation unit tests. Zero malformed payloads served.

In-scope production files at 100% line coverage at baseline (redis-store.ts 65/65, redis-monitoring-publisher.ts 119/119) and unchanged by this test-only delta (artifacts/coverage/IFC-214-before.json). Diff is test code.

notes:
IFC-214 (Redis-backed AI Monitoring State Bridge) — GENUINE exec, scoped to the one real residual gap. Phase-0 exploration established that the bridge itself was already implemented + merged under 'feat(IFC-212): Redis monitoring snapshot bridge (#59)' (commit 1d90766e6): router reads via RedisAIMonitoringStore with DB fall-through, ai-worker publishes tenant-scoped versioned snapshots, R-016 controls present. The orchestrator's 'not on main' was a `git log --grep=IFC-214` artifact (code shipped under the #59 commit title). A 2026-06-15 reconcile attestation had false-claimed COMPLETE via an IFC-212 conflation while citing a cross-process test that — as this session proved — ran in NO gate.

The sole genuine residual gap (Gap A): the DoD's cross-process integration test was excluded from every gate (apps/api/vitest.config.ts integration-suffix exclude; not under tests/integration), so it verified nothing. This PR relocates it into the CI Integration Tests lane AND strengthens it to drive the REAL publisher->Redis->real store (a true worker->API check, possible because the integration lane may import across the api/ai-worker boundary). The orphaned apps/api test is deleted; ioredis is added as a root devDependency; the Lane-I dependency-chain marker is flipped to done.

Gap B (ai-specialist 'drift/hallucination dead Redis path') was investigated and DROPPED before any code: the limit->DB bypass is a deliberate Lens-1 decision with regression tests; reversing it would re-introduce a query-semantic inconsistency (DB all-time vs publisher rolling window). No production source changed (redis-store.ts/publisher.ts SHA256 identical to #59). The CSV flip is the orchestrator's job and was NOT performed here. Full candid session log: docs/operations/sprint-18-ifc-214-session-issues-log.md.
